message:
perf(mvcc): upgrade ChainHeadShard directory from Mutex to RwLock

The `directory` field in `ChainHeadShard` maps page numbers to slot
indices and is read far more often than it is written (writes only
happen when a page is first registered via `ensure_slot`). Using a
`Mutex` forced every concurrent reader -- including hot-path lookups
in `slot_index`, the diagnostic iterator `for_each_head`, and the
`page_count` scan -- to serialize behind a single lock.

Replace the `Mutex<HashMap<...>>` with `RwLock<HashMap<...>>` so that:
- `slot_index` and the fast path of `ensure_slot` acquire a shared read
  lock, allowing full concurrency among readers.
- `for_each_head` and `page_count` (diagnostics/sampling paths) also
  take only a read lock, removing contention with the MVCC hot path.
- Only the slow (allocation) path of `ensure_slot` acquires a write
  lock, which is infrequent after warm-up.

This should reduce lock contention proportionally to the reader fanout
under concurrent MVCC snapshot lookups.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
